hostel.description: The Institute is fully residential and hostel accommodation is provided to all students. There are eleven hostels for boys and one hostel complex for girls. The hostel rooms are fully furnished. Common room facilities with Cable TV and telephone are available in each hostel. Internet connectivity has been provided in all hostel rooms. Solar water heating systems are provided in all the hostels. There is a common kitchen cum mess unit for every set of two boys hostels. Girls hostel, however, has a separate mess of its own, situated within the boundary of the hostel. It is mandatory for the students staying in the hostel to have their food in the hostel messes. The messes attached to hostels are fully managed by the students.

library.description: Library has room for up to 8,000 students, offers 738 open access study places and 246 open-access IT work stations.  As well as the books and periodicals you expect, it is home to computing facilities for students.The library is searchable via the library catalogue.  But it is not only our library you will have access to – as a BITS Pilani student you can use a network of other libraries via our membership.

sports.description: Available Facilities: Swimming Pool, Football, Hockey, Cricket, Basketball, Volleyball, Tennis, Badminton, Table Tennis, Athletics, Water Polo, Weight Lifting

reviews.batch: Batch of 2030Offered by BITS Pilani, Hyderabad CampusReviewed on 28 Nov 2025)
reviews.review_heading: It provides freedom to explore personal interests.
reviews.review_text: Hostels at BITS are clean and spacious. Each hostel also includes common rooms with indoor games like air hockey and table tennis, and outside courts for badminton and volleyball. Mess food is a well-balanced meal and made in hygienic places. The medical centre is open 24/7, and also ambulances come to the hostel gate in case of emergency. Courts and other playing facilities are well-maintained for every sport.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2028Offered by BITS Pilani, Hyderabad CampusReviewed on 4 Sep 2025)
reviews.review_heading: Worth the high fees due to ROI, but after entering, hard work is expected to excel.
reviews.review_text: Entire campus is Wi-Fi covered, and classrooms are well maintained with blocks assigned to departments like mechanical, CS, and electrical. Library is also pretty big, with a capacity of 600+, and it has a great interior, and provides a peaceful vibe for studying. Quality of hostel and mess is also good, food taste is decent with a different menu.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2027Offered by BITS Pilani, Hyderabad CampusReviewed on 16 Jun 2025)
reviews.review_heading: The college has good infrastructure, environment, facilities, and placement prospects.
reviews.review_text: Ethernet connectivity is available in hostel rooms and the library (1 Gbps speed), with Wi-Fi throughout the hostel and academic block, and in the library (100 Mbps). The food can be considered fair, with the menu undergoing slight changes every two months. For the mechanical department, we have dedicated labs, workshops, and sandbox areas.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2028Offered by BITS Pilani K K Birla, Goa CampusReviewed on 12 Feb 2025)
reviews.review_heading: Review of BITS Pilani K K Birla, Goa Campus.
reviews.review_text: The facility is very good. The academic block is beautiful, consisting of 4 lecture theatres and a lot of classrooms. Some labs are also present. A big canteen includes a kitchen and a Nescafe shop. There is also a big auditorium in the basement. There is a football and cricket ground with floodlights. Other sports facilities are also available. Hostels of the new block are very good with fully furnished rooms and 2 common washrooms on each floor. The campus is beautiful and green.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2027Offered by BITS Pilani, Hyderabad CampusReviewed on 11 Feb 2025)
reviews.review_heading: The overall experience with college is really good and I will definitely recommend it to others.
reviews.review_text: BITS is a fully residential campus with state-of-the-art infrastructure, including well-equipped laboratories, modern classrooms, a gymnasium, a swimming pool, various sports grounds, and campus-wide internet connectivity. The labs and classrooms are good.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2028Offered by BITS Pilani, Hyderabad CampusReviewed on 24 Jan 2025)
reviews.review_heading: Ups and downs of BITS through some unknown insights.
reviews.review_text: The Wi-Fi facility in our hostel is not good. The land cables work better than the Wi-Fi. Classrooms are neat and tidy. Hostels are clean, but there is a dumping ground near, which sometimes ends with a bad odour. The mess food is better than most of the colleges, and the canteen is affordable. Sports facilities are quite good and almost every sports facility is available. We all get medical insurance.

reviews.review_heading: Worth it to take admission here.
reviews.review_text: Facilities and infrastructure of this college are awesome. Wi-Fi is available throughout the college campus, classrooms, library, and labs. The quality of food available in the mess and canteen is top-notch. Medical facilities are also available 24/7, and sports and games are also good.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2024Offered by BITS Pilani K K Birla, Goa CampusReviewed on 2 Nov 2024)
reviews.review_heading: One of the best engineering colleges in India.
reviews.review_text: The course and hostel are absolutely amazing, other than one big downside, which is the food. The food is horrendous, and you eat it just for the sake of staying alive. The paid canteens are good, but the mess is bad. All classrooms are air-conditioned, with Wi-Fi throughout the campus. The library has a wide range of books to cover every genre. Sports and games are widely encouraged, and there are multiple football grounds, as well as a gym and badminton/tennis courts.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2028Offered by BITS Pilani K K Birla, Goa CampusReviewed on 28 Oct 2024)
reviews.review_heading: BITS Pilani - It's Magic.
reviews.review_text: We have great infrastructure at BITS Goa. The Goa campus is really beautiful, full of greenery, and Goa itself is also a very beautiful state. The campus is very clean, with almost no littering on the roads. Spread over 180 acres, it’s smaller than Pilani and Hyderabad, but you still have to walk a lot. Generally, first-year students are given double-occupancy rooms. Each room is equipped with a separate bed, table, chair, fan, windows, almirah, and whiteboards. Each hostel has a common room, a ...

reviews.batch: Batch of 2028Offered by BITS Pilani K K Birla, Goa CampusReviewed on 18 Oct 2024)
reviews.review_heading: Best academics as well as fest culture with good placements.
reviews.review_text:
We have washing machines.
Clean bathrooms with hot water facilities 24x7, with proper purified drinking water. 
The mess provides very good quality food. The draw back being you have to follow same mess time table (weekly) for a month or two but overall experience being awesome.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2028Offered by BITS Pilani K K Birla, Goa CampusReviewed on 11 Oct 2024)
reviews.review_heading: An Overview: Birla Institute of Technology and Science Pilani, KK Birla Goa campus
reviews.review_text: The facilities and infrastructure available in my department are top-class .  The quality of food is mostly good, but then if you choose a mess, it is comparatively better than a d mess. So, as the college gives us the option to choose the one we want, we can choose the best mess.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2028Offered by BITS Pilani, Hyderabad CampusReviewed on 27 Sep 2024)
reviews.review_heading: Our college provides a vibrant life for students and very good career opportunities.
reviews.review_text: The infrastructure of our college is very good. As it is a private college, they collect a good amount of fees to maintain hostels. Wi-Fi is provided to every student with a limit of 10 GB per day. LAN is provided in every room. Classrooms have air conditioners. They are good. The mess is good. The menu keeps changing frequently. Sports facilities are very good.

reviews.review_heading: Satisfied by its performance in placements, good campus life, and personality development!
reviews.review_text: Labs are pretty good and well-maintained. BITS Wi-Fi also works efficiently. Classrooms are spacious and air-conditioned. The library is a very nice place to study at during summers as hostel rooms essentially do not have air-conditioning. Food in the mess is vegetarian with non-veg available on Sundays.

reviews.review_heading: A really good place to develop your personality as a whole.
reviews.review_text: The classrooms are well furnished having fully functional AC, efficient lighting and seating places. Labs in the first year are somewhat renovated but the research labs are very advanced and innovative. Wi-Fi works good, just some productive services like Netflix and WhatsApp video calls are banned. The Library is very spacious and has separate places and tables for studying and books. It also has a relaxing space to sit and has facilities like coffee, tea and snacks from vending machines which ...

reviews.batch: Batch of 2026Offered by BITS Pilani K K Birla, Goa CampusReviewed on 5 Jun 2024)
reviews.review_heading: About moderate good placement season in 2023.
reviews.review_text: Wi-Fi, classroom labs, and library are of high standards, probably among the best compared to other institutions in our country. The quality of food is decent and manageable. Facilities such as sports and medical services are also very good and comfortable.

overview_faqs.question: What is the fee range for the courses offered at SGPGI, Lucknow?
overview_faqs.answer: The Sanjay Gandhi Postgraduate Institute of Medical Sciences (SGPGIMS) in Lucknow has a fee structure which varies depending on the program. B.Sc. courses range from 2L-2.2L, BPT course is 2.2L, M.Sc. courses are 1.8L and MD course is 1.1L.In totality, the fee for all the courses offered here ranges between INR 27,000 and INR 220,000.

overview_faqs.question: Is Sanjay Gandhi Postgraduate Institute of Medical Sciences, Lucknow good for B.Sc?
overview_faqs.answer: Yes, Sanjay Gandhi Post Graduate Studies, Lucknow (SGPGIMS) is eligible for B.Sc. The institute offers a four-year undergraduate programme in various disciplines including biochemistry, microbiology, physiology, anatomy, pathology, forensics, community medicine and pharmacology. The annual fee for the first year of the B.Sc programme is INR 79,800. For admission to B.Sc programme, candidates must pass 10+2 or equivalent examination with PCB and English as compulsory subjects and after that perform well in institute wise entrance examination. Overall, SGPGIMS is considered a reputed undergraduate institution that offers comprehensive curricula and excellent learning environments.

description: SGPGI cutoff 2025 has been released for NEET PG Round 2. The cutoff is out in form of ranks for admission to the MD, MS, and PG Diploma courses at the PG level. Students can view the exam-wise sections below to know more about the cutoff in detail across different courses, rounds, and categories. More exam-related updates are below:

SGPGI cut off 2025 will be released soon based on NEET PG exam results. SGP Institute of Medical Sciences accepts NEET PG scores for admission to the MD, MS and Diploma courses. Candidates can find the category-wise Round 1 and Last Round Cutoff rank for the last 4 years in the table below:
The 3rd round of seat allotment for SGPGI NEET PG cutoff 2025 has been announced for MD programmes for various categories under the All India quota.

For the General AI category, closing ranks varied from 10399 to 41755, with MD in Anaesthesiology being the most challenging for admission. In 2024 and 2023, the same specialisation had a cutoff of 10861 and 15157, respectively. A multi-year comparison shows a drop in the cutoff trends for this category. To see more information, students can check o

SGPGI accepts the NEET PG results for admission to PG-level medical courses, like MD course. For the MD specialisations, the most challenging one was MD in Anaesthesiology, with a round 3 closing rank of 10399 for the General AI category. In contrast, the cutoff for the OBC AI category for the same specialisation was 22709.
This difference between the two ranks suggests that the competition for the General AI category is lower than the OBC AI category. Students can refer to the table below to k
Students who appeared for the NEET PG exam 2025 can refer to the table below to view the NEET PG Round 1 cutoff list. They can also download the results for later use.
From the table below, students can do a comparison of different colleges that accept NEET PG results for admission.

SGPGI NEET SS Cutoff 2024 was released for the concluding round in the form of ranks for admission to DM, M.Ch. courses across multiple specialisations. The cutoff was released for various categories belonging to the AI quota.

While for the last round, the closing ranks ranged from 12 to 3938, wherein DM in Clinical Immunology proved to be the most sought-after in the General AI category. Students can check the first and last round closing ranks from the table provided below.

In this section, candidates can find out the category-wise NEET SS cutoff 2024 and 2023. In 2024, for the General AI quota, the NEET SS cutoff was closed at 12 for DM in Clinical Immunology. In 2023, the same specialisation cutoff was capped at 26.
For more in-depth details for the previous year cutoffs, please refer to the table provided below and make use of the filters available to refine your search on the basis of rounds and category.

Students can view or download the round and year-wise consolidated NEET SS cutoff PDFs for Sanjay Gandhi Postgraduate Institute of Medical Sciences. This will help students to understand the year-wise cutoff trends.

reviews_list.batch_info: MD in Psychiatry - Batch of 2024
reviews_list.review_title: My honest review of my college.

review_content.Placements: The placement cell was too good. The students had good chances of placement by the college. Most of my batchmates including me as well placed in the top hospital. The college also provided internships for the students. It provided the best company for placements and offered internships every 2 years.
review_content.Infrastructure: Now hostel building had been transformed into administrative block. There were no facilities in hostel. But there was a college canteen inside the college premises.
review_content.Faculty: Faculty members were well-experienced and they all had vast knowledge in their particular field of interest. They helped students too much in their educational life. They gave valuable advice to the students for their betterment. The course curriculum was updated and modern the medical council of India. It made students ready for their placements. The exam pattern was divided into internal and external which was held every year. In 1 year 6 subjects, 2 year 12 subjects were there which was not easy, and 3 year 8 subjects which were related to courses. Last year, there were 6-8 subjects. Faculty members were too good but you had to clear your exam on your own preparation.

reviews_list.batch_info: M.Phil. in Psychiatric Social Work - Batch of 2021
reviews_list.review_title: Review of National Institute of Mental Health and Neurosciences.

review_content.Placements: Internship and placement are both given importance in the college. The placement of students is good numbers and internship opportunities are available in both public and private hospitals. The package given to the students goes on an average of Rs. 25000 per month. There are amazing job opportunities for the students to make them learn the implementation of their learning and knowledge, and also help them gain professional experience. Various firms hire the students.
review_content.Infrastructure: M.Phil. Clinical Psychology is a doctorate-level programme which has a duration of 1 year. The structure of the course is very interesting and also innovative, with a focus on research and practical. The days and exams are tiring. The hostel facilities are not very good. It is with basic needs - internet, TV, washing machines, and water purifiers. The food in the mess is not good, although the contractor is renewing it every year, but the food quality doesn't improve.

faqs.answer: Admissions begin at NIMHANS Bengaluru 2024 NIMHANS B.Sc. Admission. Admissions are held once a year for the B.Sc. in Neurophysiology Technology program offered by NIMHANS. The admissions process typically begins in May or June, and candidates are chosen based on their performance on an entrance exam and an interview.
faqs.question: Q: When do admissions begin at NIMHANS Bengaluru?

faqs.answer: NIMHANS Bangalore has an entrance based admission process. The selection criteria is clearing the entrance exam specified according to the preferred course. Make sure that the minimum eligibility requirement is met before applying for the accepted entrance examination.
faqs.question: Q: Does NIMHANS Bangalore have an entrance or merit-based admission process?

faqs.answer: The National Institute of Mental Health and Neurosciences is also known as NIMHANS. National Institute of Mental Health and Neurosciences (NIMHANS), Bangalore was declared as an Institute of National Importance by an Act of Parliament. NIMHANS was ranked 4 under the Medical category by NIRF in 2021. The institute offers a list of courses at the UG, PG, Super Speciality and PhD levels across various specialisations. Candidate must have a minimum of 45% in their combined 10+2 exams in Physics, Chemistry, Biology, Mathematics, and English in order to be considered for the B.Sc. programmes at NIMHANS Bangalore. The selection process at NIMHANS for both undergraduate and postgraduate programs relies on the INI-CET, INI SS-ET, and NIMHANS admission tests as the foundational assessment methods.
faqs.question: Q: What is the eligibility criteria for BSc at NIMHANS?

faqs.answer: National Institute of Mental Health and Neurosciences has below criteria for taking admissions to it's various courses:CoursesEligibilityDMCandidate must have completed MBBS from University recognized by the National Medical Commission/Medical Council of India as notified by Govt. of India with mentioned marks. Candidate must have completed the required period of 12 months compulsory rotating Internship/Practical training.M.Ch.Candidate must have completed MBBS from University recognized by the National Medical Commission/Medical Council of India as notified by Govt. of India with mentioned marks. Candidate must have completed the required period of 12 months compulsory rotating Internship/Practical training.M.Sc.The candidate should be a Registered Nurse and Registered Midwife or equivalent with any State Nursing Registration Council. Candidate shall be medically fit.B.Sc.Any equivalent examination recognized by NIMHANS, Bengaluru for the above purpose with Physics, Chemistry, Biology and Mathematics as principal subjects of study with 45% aggregate. OR Job oriented Diploma course from a recognized board with Diagnostic Radiography as vocational subject or equivalent.MDCandidate must have completed the required period of 12 months compulsory rotating Internship/Practical training.CertificateB.Sc. with MLT OR B.Sc. with Life Sciences or DMLT (with one year experience).PG DiplomaCandidates should be registered nurse (RN & RM) in the state nursing council with one year experience as staff nurse. There is no entrance test for this course. The course starts in July and ends in June.MPHMBBS OR Bachelor of Dental Sciences OR Postgraduate degree in Sociology/Statistics / Environmental Sciences /Physiotherapy /Occupational Therapy / Health Management / Social Work / Economics / Speech Pathology and Audiology / Speech Pathology / Audiology / Nursing.UG DiplomaCandidate must have completed 10+2 from any recognised Board/Council.

overview_faqs.question: What is National Institute of Mental Health and Neurosciences famous for?
overview_faqs.answer: NIMHANS Bangalore is famous for its popular programmes which are MD & DM. MD & DM students are today working in top organisations, hospitals, etc. Some of them have even started their own clinical practice. Some of the alumni are today working as doctors in the NIMHANS Bangalore attached hospital. NIMHANS Medical College Bangalore conducts workshops like the 9th National Workshop on Research Methodology in Psychosocial Issues.

review_summary.college_name: NIMHANS Bangalore Reviews on Placements, Faculty & Facilities

individual_reviews.course: M.Phil. in Clinical Psychology - Batch of 2026
individual_reviews.title: Absolutely I am satisfied with the faculty members, placements, etc.

content.Placements: The placements from this course are generally strong and respected due to the prestige of NIMHANS. Top recruiting companies/hospitals that offered jobs are government or private psychiatric hospitals, NGOs like Banyan, Sangath, etc. Top roles offered are psychiatric social worker, lecturer/assistant professor, researcher, etc.
content.Infrastructure: Infrastructure for the department of psychiatry social workers has a dedicated department building with classroom, seminar hall, and faculty office. Smart classroom, well-stocked library, seminars, workshops, and clubs are also available. Quality of food is good, and separate hostels are available on campus.

individual_reviews.course: M.Phil. in Clinical Psychology - Batch of 2026
individual_reviews.title: I'm absolutely satisfied with the placement and college infrastructure.

content.Placements: 90% of students are placed in this course during their placements. The highest package is 20 LPA, the average is 10 LPA, and the lowest was 5 LPA. Many top recruiting companies offer jobs. Top roles offered include neurology, physiology, and other areas.
content.Infrastructure: Facilities and infrastructure are too good, and the campus is too big. In every class, projectors and air-conditioners are present, and on campus, Wi-Fi and a well-stocked library are available. The quality of food is average, and it's made with hygiene and is healthy.

individual_reviews.course: MD in Psychiatry - Batch of 2024
individual_reviews.title: My honest review of my college.

content.Placements: The placement cell was too good. The students had good chances of placement by the college. Most of my batchmates including me as well placed in the top hospital. The college also provided internships for the students. It provided the best company for placements and offered internships every 2 years.
content.Infrastructure: Now hostel building had been transformed into administrative block. There were no facilities in hostel. But there was a college canteen inside the college premises.
content.Faculty: Faculty members were well-experienced and they all had vast knowledge in their particular field of interest. They helped students too much in their educational life. They gave valuable advice to the students for their betterment. The course curriculum was updated and modern the medical council of India. It made students ready for their placements. The exam pattern was divided into internal and external which was held every year. In 1 year 6 subjects, 2 year 12 subjects were there which was not easy, and 3 year 8 subjects which were related to courses. Last year, there were 6-8 subjects. Faculty members were too good but you had to clear your exam on your own preparation.

individual_reviews.course: M.Phil. in Psychiatric Social Work - Batch of 2021
individual_reviews.title: Review of National Institute of Mental Health and Neurosciences.

content.Placements: Internship and placement are both given importance in the college. The placement of students is good numbers and internship opportunities are available in both public and private hospitals. The package given to the students goes on an average of Rs. 25000 per month. There are amazing job opportunities for the students to make them learn the implementation of their learning and knowledge, and also help them gain professional experience. Various firms hire the students.
content.Infrastructure: M.Phil. Clinical Psychology is a doctorate-level programme which has a duration of 1 year. The structure of the course is very interesting and also innovative, with a focus on research and practical. The days and exams are tiring. The hostel facilities are not very good. It is with basic needs - internet, TV, washing machines, and water purifiers. The food in the mess is not good, although the contractor is renewing it every year, but the food quality doesn't improve.

individual_reviews.course: Discontinued (FEB 2026) B.Sc. in Anaesthesia Technology - Batch of 2023
individual_reviews.title: B.Sc. in Anaesthesia Technology at NIMHANS.

content.Placements: Around 90% of students have been placed. Even though there is no placement cell, because of the name and pride of such a prestigious institution getting placed is not that hard task. Students are mostly placed in AIIMS, government hospitals, private hospitals, and outside India like NHS, etc.
content.Infrastructure: Infrastructure that they provide is top-notch. No other institute provides such amenities for patient services. NIMHANS is a frontline runner in the medical field, which always chooses and makes innovations in the medical field. Classrooms are smart classes with an updated curriculum. In hostel, we get a single room with Wi-Fi facilities and 24/7 security. There are 2 grounds and one gymkhana.

faqs.question: What is the average package offered at NIMHANS Bengaluru?
faqs.answer: Impressive results in both UG and PG placements have been attained by NIMHANS Bangalore Placements 2023. The institute has consistently maintained placements in the three-year UG programmes. All graduates found employment in 2021, with the median wage at NIMHANS being INR 6,00,000. Similarly, 14 students were placed each year in 2020 and 2019, with a median pay of INR 6,00,000. The institute has obtained outstanding outcomes for the 4-year B.Sc. Nursing programme. Out of the 77 seats that were open in 2021, 72 students were placed, with the median pay at NIMHANS being INR 7,20,000. Similar to how 74 and 75 students were placed in 2020 and 2019, respectively, with the identical seat intake. The median annual pay for each of them was INR 7,20,000. Regarding the one-year PG programme, NIMHANS placements 2023 have displayed exceptional successes. Out of 98 available seats in 2020, 33 students were placed, earning a median income of INR 15,60,000. With 78 seats available in 2019, 25 students were placed successfully, earning a median income of INR 6,00,000.

faqs.question: Does NIMHANS Bengaluru offer better placements than Vydehi Institute of Medical Sciences and Research Centre?
faqs.answer: NIMHANS Bangalore and Vydehi Institute of Medical Sciences and Research Centre are both considered top medical colleges in Bangalore. Out of 77 students, NIMHANS Bangalore placed 72 in 2021. The 2021 batch's median pay was INR 7,20,000. For 3-year UG students, the average package was INR 6, while for 4-year UG students, it was INR 7.20. Nearly 30% of the students of Vydehi Institute of Medical Sciences and Research Centre were placed. Students from this college are hired by leading recruiting companies. So choice is yours.

For the same course, the OBC AI category cutoff was capped at 1047. This difference in the ranks signifies that the competition for the General AI category is higher than the OBC AI quota in the second round. Students can see the table below and use t

NIMHANS accepts INI CET scores, followed by a counselling process for admission to the MD, MS, and other courses. The INI CET 2026 Round 1 cutoff is out for the January session. reviews_list.review_title: Absolutely satisfied with my college IIT BHU because of facilities and good placement in 2026.

review_content.Placements: In the electronics branch, the placement percentage is approximately 81 percent. The highest package is 90 LPA, the lowest is 7.5 LPA, and the average is 23.5 LPA. Top companies are Google, Microsoft, and Nvidia. 40 percent of students got internships in core jobs, where the major company was Nvidia, and a few also got internships in SDE. Roles include VLSI, embedded systems, and PCB.
review_content.Infrastructure: The facilities are very good at IIT BHU. Wi-Fi covers the entire campus, and classrooms are fully modernized. There are 4 lecture theatres in our college. For freshers, IIT BHU has the best hostel among all IITs, named PC Ray Hostel, which covers all required infrastructure and is newly made, so it looks aesthetic as well. There are 3 huge grounds in IIT BHU, which cover facilities for all sports. We have SAC for indoor games and a newly made gym, which is the biggest sports auditorium as well.

reviews_list.review_title: Our college is the best for serious students.

review_content.Placements: There were very few placement offers, because most of the students preferred higher studies in their respective fields. review_content.Infrastructure: There was a decent hostel in our college, which was not too good, but there were some basic facilities provided by the hostel, like free Wi-Fi, and there was also a place for playing outdoor games. individual_reviews.course: B.Sc.(Hons) in Chemistry with Botany - Batch of 2027
individual_reviews.title: College placement is not so good, their percentage is very low.

content.Placements: The strength of my class is 79. Almost all students get an internship. Colleges offer students the opportunity to got internship from another college or industry. If they do not approach any industry or college, then the college itself provides the internship. The average package forwarded in my courses is Rs. 50000 per month.
content.Infrastructure: Lab in the first and 2nd semester is done once a week, and in the third semester it is twice a week, and in the 4th semester it is 4 days a week. There are two batches of Chemistry. The maximum number of students doing lab together is 30 or more.

individual_reviews.course: B.Tech. in Electrical Engineering - Batch of 2029
individual_reviews.title: If you want a good package, then you should join this college.

content.Placements: Almost 95-96 percent of students in electrical have been placed. The highest package given is 1.2 crore per annum, which was in the field of cybersecurity. The lowest is around 12 LPA, and the average package is around 27 LPA, while the median is around 30 LPA. Top recruiting companies include Texas Instruments, Microsoft, and Nvidia. Around 50 percent of students from electrical engineering grab an internship. Specifically, this year, 55 students got an internship. [8 core and rest non-core ] Top roles include SDE, cybersecurity, VLSI designer, etc.
content.Infrastructure: Sometimes there is a problem with Wi-Fi connectivity in our hostel rooms. The library is very good, and it contains all types of books that are required. Classrooms are okay. Labs are very good, but some of the equipment has mechanical errors. The medical facility is excellent. Food quality depends on which type of mess you have joined, like in a sattvik mess, where Jain food is served, the quality of food is excellent compared to other messes. The sports facility is also very good. The canteen also provides good food.

paragraphs: BHU admissionsare primarily entrance-based. All Indian applicants are required to take the CUET-UG exam conducted by the NTA. Candidates must apply online on the NTA CUET official website to register themselves. For PG courses, aspirants with a valid CUET-PG score can apply. Admission to some courses can be merit-based. BHU considers CUET-UG scores and normalised scores to create merit lists for each course.
What is the BHU Application Process 2026?

For UG courses admission through CUET, candidates have to complete theCUET-UG application process. Likewise, forBHU PG admissions, theCUET-PG applicationprocess has to be completed. For other courses, the aspirants can apply through BHU official website.

BHU counselling process for PG courses through CUET-PG is carried out in online mode. Registrations for it get started before CUET results are out and open after that for scorecard upload. Find below the detailed procedure of BHU CUET-PG counselling:
1 -Register on theBHU admission portal

Visit the university's official website(bhuonline.in)to participate in the online counselling process. To register, enter yourNTA application number, and other details along with your email ID and a passwordto activate the registration account.

Login with the Email ID and password entered during registration. After login, fill out the application and upload documents. Candidates must upload the documents inPDF/JPEG format. Moreover, the required documents are enlisted below:
Matriculation or its equivalent certificate
Class 12 mark sheet
Bachelor's mark sheet (make a single file of marks sheet of all semesters and then upload the same as a single document)
Caste certificate (SC/ST/OBC/EWS) and income certificate (SC/ST)
PwD certificate (if applicable)
BHU employee certificate (if applicable)
Certificate if admission under sports quota
Scanned copy of a recent colour photograph & signature
3 -Pay the BHU Counselling Registration Fee

In order to register successfully, anon-refundablefee has to be paid via debit card/credit card/net banking. Candidates canedit the application before fee payment. Find below the registration fee for each of the categories:
4 -Preference Entry Page

The preference entry page opens after the registration fee payment. Candidates can fill in their preferences separately for all the programmes in this form. Moreover, the result for the first seat allotment is declared within 8 to 10 days (approximately) after the publication of theCUET-PG result. Further, subsequent rounds for regular/paid fee seats for the BHU and affiliated colleges are carried out.

BHU feesinclude various components such as tuition fees, hostel fees, security deposit, library fees, etc. The fee payment shall be accepted annually or semester-wise. BHU fee structure varies depending upon the chosen course. It also varies for regular and paid seats. Candidates wanting to apply for afee refund from BHUhave to fill out a form. Students can download the new BHU fee refund formhere. The table given below mentions theBHU feesof some of the courses:
NOTE:The aforementioned course fee is as per the official website/sanctioning body. It is subject to changes and hence is indicative.

The seat reservation at BHU is provided under different categories. The reservation is given by the university and under the provision of the government of India/UGC guidelines. The university provides seat reservations toSC/ST/OBC/Physically Challenged. Moreover, the university has also paid seats (a maximum of 15% of the total seats). Candidates must have the caste supporting documents to get a reserved seat. Tabulated below is theBHU seat intakefor some of its courses:
Popular Courses
Seat Intake

BSc and BSc Hons
NOTE:The above-mentioned seat intake is as per the official website/sanctioning body. It is subject to changes and hence is indicative.

faqs.question: Does BHU accept CUET?
faqs.answer: Yes, Banaras Hindu University accept CUET score for multiple UG courses, including BA (Hons), BCom (Hons) BSc (Hons), etc. The university is also offering admission to its PG courses based on merit in CUET-PG. Aspirants who meet the eligibility requirements can register on the BHU admission portal for counselling. The admission is done in order of merit based on the normalised score of the CUET (PG) and reservation policy as per the provisions laid down in the BHU PET Information Brochure.

faqs.question: What is the admission process for the BHU UET?
faqs.answer: BHU accepts CUET-UG scores for admission to several UG courses. Candidates seeking admission to BHU must apply online to register themselves. Follow the step-wise admission process as given below:The BHU application process is conducted in online mode through the official website of NTA (nta.ac.in). The fee for General category students is INR 750, INR 700 for OBC-NCL/EWS, and INR 650 for SC/ST/PwD/Transgender.Candidates need to download the admit card by visiting the official website of CUET 2025 and clicking on 'Download CUET Admit Card'.After the declaration of the CUET result, students seeking admission to BHU UG courses are required to apply online for CUET-UG Counselling on the BHU UG admission portal.Further, candidates are required to participate in an online counselling process for provisional admission to the course (s). After that, the final seat allotment will be done by BHU.

faqs.question: What is the last date for BHU admission?
faqs.answer: BHU accepts CUET scores for admission to UG and PG courses. The admissions are over within few weeks of CUET counselling. CUET-PG is usually jeld in March whereas, CUET-UG takes place in May. Students must check the complete schedule for exact dates. CUET applications for the coming year will begin in February and continue till April.

faqs.question: Can a one-year dropper student get admission into BHU?
faqs.answer: Banaras Hindu University course admissions is entrance-based for most of the programmes. Thus, one-year dropper student can apply for the desired course, however, qualifying the respective entrance is the a must. Apart from this, aspirant should meet the course-specific minimum eligibility requirements as well.Moreover, the student can be asked to submit an application regarding the reason for drop out. Sometimes even this is not required.

placement_report.college_name: Banaras Hindu University (BHU) Placements 2025: Highest Package, Average Package, Top Recruiters

placement_data.title: Banaras Hindu University Placement Overview 2025
paragraphs: The highest and average package recorded during BHU MBA & MBA IB placements 2025 were INR 19.8 LPA and INR 10.1 LPA , respectively. For MBA Agri-Business course, the highest and average package recorded this year were INR 16 LPA and INR 8.7 LPA . During Banaras Hindu University MBA and MBA IB placements 2025, most number of profiles offered was of Consultant whereas for MBA Agri-Business program, Management Trainee emerged as the top profile.

According to the NIRF report 2025 released the median package offered to UG 3 year was INR 8 LPA during the BHU placements 2024. Moreover, the median packages offered to UG 4 Year and UG 5 Year were INR 9 LPA and INR 16 LPA , respectively. Further, the median package received during BHU placement 2024 for PG 2-year and PG 3-year programs were INR 9 LPA and INR 13.5 LPA , respectively.
Check out more on Highlights in the below video:

faqs.question: What is the hostel fee at BHU, including mess charge and everything?
faqs.answer: The hostel fee structure for BHU is INR 3,000 - INR 4,000 per semester depending upon the degree. The hostel fee does not include mess charges. Banaras Hindu University is spread across a 1,300-acre campus. There are 41 hostels for boys and 21 for girls. Additionally, there is separate accommodation for international students. All the hostels have basic amenities like water purifiers, a common room, recreation room, mess, TV, laundry services, security and surveillance system, etc.

highlights.description:
One of India’s leading institutes is BHU. It has been consistently recognised by numerous national & international ranking agencies. As per the NIRF 2025, the institute ranked #10 under Overall category, #6 under University, #60 under Management, #6 under Medical, and #15 under Dental. Internationally, QS World 2025 has also ranked the institute #1001-1200. BHU excellence in academics, distinguished professors, and well-established infrastructure is the reason for its high ranks among numerous ranking agencies. All the information around BHU rankings is covered in the following article:
Read less

nirf.description:
BHU has received strong ranks by the NIRF under the 'Overall' category. Provided parameters including, Teaching, Graduation Outcome, Inclusivity and Research, the institute is ranked in a good position as compared to other institutes by the NIRF 2025. In the 'Overall' category, the institute's ranks have shown a fluctuation when compared with the previous year's ranking. In 2025, BHU climbed 1 rank to #10 rank in the 'Overall' category by the NIRF ranking.
One can observe that within the category of 'University', the institute's ranking has fluctuation when compared with the previous year's ranking. In 2025, BHU fell 1 rank to #6 rank in the 'University' category by the NIRF ranking.
It is to be noticed that the institute has shown a fluctuating trend in the 'Management' category as compared to the ranking of preceding years. In 2025, BHU fell 12 ranks to #60 rank in the 'Management' category by the NIRF ranking.
It is noticeable that in the 'Medical' category, the institute's ranks have shown an increasing trend when evaluated against the ranking of the prior year. In 2025, BHU climbed 1 rank to #6 rank in the 'Medical' category by the NIRF ranking.
Find below the latest rankings and category breakdowns from several ranking bodies.
Read less

outlook.description:
BHU has been recognised for its excellence in various fields by Outlook 2025, outperforming other institutes in parameters such as Teaching, Graduation Outcome, Inclusivity & Research, as well as fostering an exceptional learning environment. In the 'Central Universities' category, the institute's ranks have displayed constancy when compared with the previous year's ranking. In 2025, BHU's rank is #2, the same as last year's rank in the 'Central Universities' category by the Outlook ranking.
Explore the BHU latest rankings by other ranking bodies and their categories below:
Read less

qs_world.description:
As per the overall score, BHU has been placed at a good position by the QS World University Rankings under the 'Top Global Universities' category, reflecting its exceptional performance in areas like Teaching, Research, Graduation Outcomes, and creating an inclusive, quality learning environment. When compared to the ranking from the previous years, it has been seen that in the 'Top Global Universities' category, the institute's ranks have shown stability in trend. In 2026, BHU's rank is #1001, the same as last year's rank in the 'Top Global Universities' category by the QS World University Rankings.
One can observe that within the category of 'Asia', the institute's ranking has fallen when compared with the previous year's ranking. In 2026, BHU fell 21 ranks to #224 rank in the 'Asia' category by the QS World University Rankings. This is the second consecutive year that the institute has slipped in the rankings.
The institute has displayed a decreasing trend in the 'Southern Asia' category as compared to the ranking of preceding years. In 2026, BHU fell 6 ranks to #44 rank in the 'Southern Asia' category by the QS World University Rankings. This is the second consecutive year that the institute has slipped in the rankings.
Check out the latest rankings for other ranking bodies and their categories below.
Read less

reviews.review_heading: College placement is not so good, their percentage is very low.
reviews.review_text: Lab in the first and 2nd semester is done once a week, and in the third semester it is twice a week, and in the 4th semester it is 4 days a week. There are two batches of Chemistry. The maximum number of students doing lab together is 30 or more.

reviews.review_heading: If you want a good package, then you should join this college.
reviews.review_text: Sometimes there is a problem with Wi-Fi connectivity in our hostel rooms. The library is very good, and it contains all types of books that are required. Classrooms are okay. Labs are very good, but some of the equipment has mechanical errors. The medical facility is excellent. Food quality depends on which type of mess you have joined, like in a sattvik mess, where Jain food is served, the quality of food is excellent compared to other messes. The sports facility is also very good. The canteen ...

reviews.batch: Batch of 2029Offered by Indian Institute of Technology, BHU, VaranasiReviewed on 21 Jan 2026)
reviews.review_heading: Absolutely satisfied with my college IIT BHU because of facilities and good placement in 2026.
reviews.review_text: The facilities are very good at IIT BHU. Wi-Fi covers the entire campus, and classrooms are fully modernized. There are 4 lecture theatres in our college. For freshers, IIT BHU has the best hostel among all IITs, named PC Ray Hostel, which covers all required infrastructure and is newly made, so it looks aesthetic as well. There are 3 huge grounds in IIT BHU, which cover facilities for all sports. We have SAC for indoor games and a newly made gym, which is the biggest sports auditorium as well.

reviews.review_heading: Our college is the best for serious students.
reviews.review_text: There was a decent hostel in our college, which was not too good, but there were some basic facilities provided by the hostel, like free Wi-Fi, and there was also a place for playing outdoor games. Overall, it was 7/10, and the infrastructure of the course was good. You don't have much pressure, you have time to explore new things and find your interests.

reviews.review_heading: Average for dairy, above average for college life.
reviews.review_text: The college has good and well-maintained infrastructure that supports both academic and extracurricular activities. Our college is big, but our department isn't; we need more strength as students and as teachers, too. Classrooms and laboratories are properly equipped. Only ICAR students can get a hostel (SRK HOSTEL).

reviews.review_heading: Mahila Maha Vidyalaya, Banaras Hindu University, is good value for money.
reviews.review_text: In Mahila Maha Vidyalaya, labs for science students are well-equipped and maintained. Classes are held regularly. There is free Wi-Fi for all students, including hostellers and day scholars. As our university is really old, some buildings have slightly degraded, but new buildings are also there with better infrastructure. Mess food and hostels are also average to above average in accordance with the fees. But one hostel is under construction in MMV as of now, so not all girls have been allotted ...

reviews.review_heading: Very good college ancient college in India.
reviews.review_text: There is a wide range of books available in our college. The variety of books is very nice. There we get books of spirituality, competitive exams, novels, biographies, self development and many more. The classrooms of our college are decent. The facilities is vey nice. The sports facilities in our college are excellent. There are 23 sports that are introduced in our college. In our college there are multiple clubs for the interests of every individual in our college. Our hostel is the newly ...

reviews.batch: Batch of 2021Offered by Indian Institute of Technology, BHU, VaranasiReviewed on 9 Jan 2026)
reviews.review_heading: IIT Bhu is a vibe, go enjoy it.
reviews.review_text: Our college has a library named the Shrinivas Despande Library. It had a vast collection of the latest and historical books. The facilities in the classroom were the latest but they do not have AC in the classroom but it contains a smart board, a green board and a projector. Our college had a gym which had the latest equipment and also three grounds. 2 volleyball court 2 lawn tennis court and many more courts for different games. Mainly, our college had a building name sac which contains all ind...

reviews.batch: Batch of 2028Offered by Indian Institute of Technology, BHU, VaranasiReviewed on 8 Jan 2026)
reviews.review_heading: Our college is nice we love it.
reviews.review_text: We have good food, a mess, Wi-Fi, a ibrary and a lot of things. The structure of labs and classrooms is old, it is already a bit old, but new halls are being built which are very good. Initially there are good facilities in the hostel but later on it happens that there is no water and the geyser is facility.

reviews.review_heading: Good in infrastructure and campus surroundings, below average in placement and teaching.
reviews.review_text: The facilities and infrastructure are quite good. It has a good library and a strong Wi-Fi connection all over the campus. But sometimes it lacks in maintaining classroom cleanliness. Hostels have exercise areas, a garden, and playing areas. The food in the mess and canteen is average. It supports sports and games.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2029Offered by Indian Institute of Technology, BHU, VaranasiReviewed on 30 Dec 2025)
reviews.review_heading: IIT BHU, The best of the most.
reviews.review_text: One thing in which my college lags is infrastructure. It is not as great as other IITs. They are working heavily on improving it. New hostels are being made. Canteen services are being improved. Wi-Fi is good. AC is also being installed.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2029Offered by Indian Institute of Technology, BHU, VaranasiReviewed on 30 Dec 2025)
reviews.review_heading: I have an amazing experience at IIT BHU Varanasi.
reviews.review_text: Infrastructure is quite good, but not so cool compared to other IITs. Day by day, infrastructure is getting better. Hostels are also getting renovated, and the new ones are quite good (for 1st and 2nd year). Mess food is obviously very good. The canteen is open till 3 AM, so it's very helpful.

reviews.review_heading: I am extremely satisfied with my faculty, course and infrastructure of the university.
reviews.review_text: Banaras Hindu University is best known for its facilities, infrastructure and brand name in the market. The classes are well-equipped with smart classes, and the library has all the facilities a student needs to study and concentrate. The campus has a Wi-Fi facility for the students. The food being provided in the mess is good and hygienic, with 3 meals and evening snacks being provided occasionally. We have medical facilities being provided, considering we already have a hospital on campus itse...

reviews.batch: Batch of 2028Offered by Indian Institute of Technology, BHU, VaranasiReviewed on 24 Dec 2025)
reviews.review_heading: College is the support for all the sports and infrastructure, and the food is good.

Wi-Fi in the classroom is somewhat limited, but in the library it's excellent. An excellent thing about this college is the support for all the sports and infrastructure, and the food is also very good, with a protein-rich diet.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2028Offered by Indian Institute of Technology, BHU, VaranasiReviewed on 20 Dec 2025)
reviews.review_heading: Overview for freshers in 2026.
reviews.review_text: The infrastructure is not very modern but its bearable. In recent months it has upgraded a lot by adding ACs and new buildings. Wi-Fi is great all over the campus from the start, hostel rooms and bathrooms are good as well for a government college. There is a variety of food in the mess at a time and the menu is not repeated all week.

college_info.college_name: Banaras Hindu University (BHU)

data.description: No, CAT is not necessary for MBA at BHU. The university offers a two-year full-time MBA course at the postgraduate level. Candidates seeking admission must visit the official website of the University (bhu.ac.in) to apply online. BHU MBA admission is based on candidates' performance in the CAT entrance test. The final selection is based on the student's performance in the GD & PI rounds. However, for admission to the MBA special courses of study, candidates can get admission with a valid CUET-PG score. Apart from this, the course aspirants must fulfil the course-specific eligibility requirements as well.

review_summarisation.heading: What students say about Banaras Hindu University

likes.text: Renowned companies like Microsoft, Amazon, Flipkart, TCS, Swiggy, and others actively recruit from our institution.
The placements in our college is excellent, a huge number of students got placed in various companies.
Majority of the students (college) went for non core job profiles ranging from analyst to operations manager to mentors ( coaching institutes).

Placements.info_text: These insights are automatically extracted from student reviews, without any manual intervention

At the PG level, the college offers three-year DM in six specialisations and MCh in eight specialisations, MD, minimum three-year PhD in seven specialisations, two-year MPH diploma, and PG diploma courses. The method of selection includes 10 marks for MBBS, 15 marks for MD/ MS/ DNB, 30 marks for theory (written) entrance examination, 30 marks for departmental clinical/ practical and viva voce, and 15 marks for the final interview. The application process for admission into the Institute for Medical Sciences and technology is done online. SCTIMST Trivandrum provides admission to diploma, P. G. D, M. D, M. PH, D. M and M. Ch. review_content.Placements: The placement rate for the 2013 PG 2 year placement was 92%. In the 2023 academic year, 100% of PG students were placed, with all 28 students successfully placed. Top recruiting companies include pharmaceutical organizations and prestigious healthcare companies such as Apollo Hospital, Boston Scientific, Johnson and Johnson, Bigstone Dickinson, Cipla, and Sun Pharma.
review_content.Infrastructure: SCTIMST has a comprehensive infrastructure designed to support its educational, research, and healthcare missions. The hospital wing has 253 beds and specializes in cardiovascular and neurological diseases. Biomedical technology is a focus, with research ongoing in the development of innovative biomedical devices. The libraries have a collection of over 25,000 books and subscribe to 170 journals, providing access to a wealth of information. Separate hostels for boys and girls are equipped with essential amenities. The campus also has an IT infrastructure, auditorium, sports facilities, Wi-Fi, and labs.

Eligible candidates must visit the institute's official website to apply. Applicants are required to appear for the Entrance Examination and Personal Interview, both of which are conducted by the institute. The final selection is based on the candidates' educational qualifications, professional experience in the area of public health, performance in the entrance test and personal interview.Shortlisted candidates must submit the first instalment of the SCTIMST MPH tuition fee, along with the other charges, such as the admission fee, security deposit, etc. Further, they must report to the campus with the required original documents for physical verification on the specified date.

faqs.answer: You cannot get direct admission in MPH course at SCTIMST. If you want admission in MPH at SCTIMST here are the eligibility criteria details: Graduates in the following fields: medicine (MBBS), dentistry (BDS), AYUSH (BAMS, BNYS, BUMS, BSMS, BHMS), B.Tech or BE (Any Branch), and four-year veterinary/nursing Science degree programs Students with a postgraduate degree in Statistics/Biostatistics, Demography, Population Studies, Nutrition, Sociology, Economics, Psychology, Anthropology, Social Work, Management, or Law, or a bachelor's degree in Physiotherapy, Occupational Therapy, or Pharmacy. It is preferable to have work experience in a health-related profession.For selection a written exam, an interview, professional experience related to the subject of public health, and educational background will all be taken into consideration . Candidates from abroad must certify their level of English proficiency.

paragraphs: As per SCTIMST Medical NIRF report 2024, the median package offered during PG 2-year and PG 3-year placements 2023 stood at INR 5.53 LPA and INR 24.25 LPA , respectively. Earlier, according to the NIRF report 2023, 68 PG (2-year) and 27 PG (3-year) students were placed during SCTIMST Trivandrum placements 2022. The median package offered during SCTIMST Trivandrum placements was INR 5.04 LPA for PG (2-year) and INR 2.64 LPA for PG (3-year) students.

tables.context: What is the salary package offered during SCTIMIST

faqs.question: How was placement at SCTIMST, Trivandrum?
faqs.answer: 87% of candidates were placed during the SCTIMST Thiruvananthapuram PG 2 Year Placements in 2021. Previously, 93% and 87% of candidates were placed in the SCTIMST Thiruvananthapuram PG 2-Year Placements in 2020 and 2019, respectively. The average package offered in Sri Chitra Thirunal Institute of Medical Sciences and Technology Thiruvananthapuram for PG 2 Year and PG 3 Year Internships in 2021 was Rs 5.1 LPA and Rs 2.4 LPA, respectively, according to SCTIMST Thiruvananthapuram NIRF Report 2022.

faqs.question: Is there any government job that I can get after completing MPH at SCTIMST Trivandrum?
faqs.answer: Yes, besides the private sector jobs, the career opportunities after pursuing an MPH include various government jobs as well. After graduating with an MPH degree from SCTIMST Trivandrum, students can build a career in the fields of public health administration, epidemiology, health policy, and health education. They can work with organisations like the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare, National Health Mission, Indian Council of Medical Research (ICMR), and State Public Health Departments. Some famous job roles may include public health officers, health policy analysts, epidemiologists, health programme managers, researchers, etc.

faqs.question: Does SCTIMST Trivandrum offer 100% placements to MPH students?
faqs.answer: SCTIMIST Trivandrum has not released the course-specific placement rate for MPH. According to SCTIMST NIRF Report 2023, the placement rate recorded during PG (2-year) placements in 2022 was 92%. Earlier, the placement rate during SCTIMST Trivandrum PG (2-year) placements in 2021 and 2020 was 87% and 93%, respectively. Hence, the institute may not have a placemet rate of 100% but has maintained a notable placement rate of over 80% for PG (2-year) courses.

faqs.question: What is the salary package offered during SCTIMIST Trivandrum MPH placements?
faqs.answer: SCTIMST Trivandrum has not yet released the official report on MPH placements 2024. However, the institute's Medical NIRF report 2024, contains the following insights on the median package offered during PG (2-year) placements in 2023 and 2022: Course Median Package in 2023 Median Package in 2022 PG (2-year) INR 5.53 LPA INR 5.04 LPA NOTE: The mentioned insights are applicable as MPH is a two-year programme offered at the PG level.

reviews_list.review_title: I am very satisfied with this college.

review_content.Placements: Around 60-70% of students got placed in this course. The highest salary package offered was 8 LPA. The lowest salary package offered was 4 LPA. Top recruiting companies are Sun Pharma, Cipla, and Mankind. Almost 40-50% of students got internships in this course.

Hostels are separate for boys and girls, with facility of gym, washing machines, and water coolers. Mess in common for both, and food quality is good.

college_info.intro:
All India Institute of Medical Sciences Jodhpur popular programmes are BSc and MBBS. BSc course is provided through the College of Nursing, AIIMS, Jodhpur which was established in 2013. Students joining the Nursing courses are well-groomed and trained by a group of experienced and well-trained faculty members. This training is at par with international standards. AIIMS Jodhpur students are taught empathy, dignity, professionalism, integrity, and compassion.
AIIMS Jodhpur MBBS students are taught about Infection Control, Air Borne Precautions, Contact Precautions, Infection Control Committee, and others. Overall, All India Institute of Medical Sciences AIIMS Jodhpur is one of the top colleges offering quality education to students. Below mentioned are some of the courses offered by AIIMS Jodhpur and their respective eligibility and AIIMS jodhpur fees:

faqs.question: What is the AIIMS Jodhpur INICET cutoff 2026? I belong to the OBC category.

faqs.answer: A:    AIIMS Jodhpur cutoff 2024 was released for MSc admission on the basis of AIIMS Nursing. individual_reviews.title: I just want to say that this college is just awesome.

content.Placements: It is not like IIT college and others. In our college, college itself is responsible for internships. All students do their internship before leaving college and in internship we also get paid like 33,000 right now and stipend is increasing year per year
content.Infrastructure: The central library is present, where every known book in MBBS is available and the digital library is also present. Classroom's infrastructure is just awesome and labs are also good and on the whole campus, high-speed Wi-Fi is available. In hostels, Wi-Fi is also available. Sports and games facilities are very good. Every known game court is present here; even an Olympic-size pool is also available. The hostel is provided with double rooms having all the facilities.

faqs.question: What is the eligibility criteria for admission to MBBS at AIIMS Jodhpur?

faqs.answer: The NeXT Exam is conducted in two separate segments knows as ‘Steps’ in the online mode. The NExT Step 1 is a theoretical examination consisting of six papers. NExT Step 2 is a Practical / Clinical and Viva Voce examination covering seven clinical subjects or disciplines. The qualifying cutoff marks in NExT Step 1 will be calculated as whole number which will be considered as a Raw Scores with appropriate decimals and subsequently corresponding Percentages. The minimum marks for passing in NeXT are 50% or half of the maximum possible Raw for NExT Step 1.

faqs.question: When did NTA release the NEET 2024 result?
faqs.answer: The NEET 2025 result is expected to be released in the first week of June 2025, within a month of conducting the exam. NTA does not announce the NEET result date in advance. Although last year the result date was stated to be declared on June 14, NTA released it 10 days in advance. Hence, there is no fixed date or schedule for NTA to declare the NEET result. Prior to the declaration of the result, NTA releases the final NEET answer key. Last year, NEET was conducted on May 5 and the result was declared June 4.

placement_data.title: AIIMS Jodhpur Placement Overview 2025
paragraphs: AIIMS Jodhpur NIRF report 2025 is released. As per the report, the median package recorded during AIIMS Jodhpur placement 2023 for UG 5-year course was INR 13.56 LPA. 61 students were placed and 30 students were selected for higher studies out of total 91 students in 2023.

PG 2-year and PG 3-year median package recorded in 2024 was INR 8.88 LPA and INR 16.5 LPA. Out of total 49 students, 37 students were placed and 3 were selected for higher studies during PG 3-year placement 2024. In PG 2-year course, 71 students were placed and 23 students went for higher studies out of 94 students.

faqs.question: How are the placements at AIIMS Jodhpur?
faqs.answer: As per the NIRF report 2025 released in the Medical category, 61 students were placed and 30 students were selected for higher studies out of total 91 students during UG 3-year placements 2023. The median package offered in 2023 was 13.56 LPA.

faqs.question: What is the total fees of MBBS at AIIMS Jodhpur?
faqs.answer: The total tuition fee for the MBBS programme at AIIMS Jodhpur is INR 24,100. This fee does not include the hostel fee or the mess fee and includes the total cost of tuition for the 5.5 years MBBS programme. The above-mentioned fee is taken from an official source/sanctioning body. Hence, it may vary. To get admission to the MBBS programme at AIIMS Jodhpur the asping candidates need to take NEET exam and the exam fee for NEET exam is INR 1,700.

description: AIIMS Jodhpur cutoff 2025 is available on this page for various accepting exams. Candidates can refer to the exam-wise sections below to view the cutoff details across different rounds and categories. Other important updates are as follows:

AIIMS Jodhpur cutoff 2024 has been released for NEET UG, detailing both opening and closing ranks across the General, OBC, SC, ST, EWS, and PWD categories. The AIIMS Jodhpur NEET UG cutoff 2025 will be published following the announcement of the NEET UG 2025 result. In the meantime, candidates seeking admission to AIIMS Jodhpur can refer to the table below to review the NEET UG and INI CET cutoff ranks for the past three years across various courses, specialisations and categories.

AIIMS Jodhpur NEET UG cutoff 2025 was concluded with the release of the final seat allotment result for admission to the MBBS course. The cutoff was released for several categories belonging to the AI quota. For the General AI quota, the NEET UG last round closing rank was 386 for MBBS admission. Candidates can refer to the table below to view the first and last round cutoffs 2025 for the General AI category students.

AIIMS Jodhpur MBBS cutoff 2025 was 386 for the students belonging to the General AI quota. Similarly, for the OBC category students, the NEET UG last round closing rank was 766 for admission to the MBBS course. Comparing both categories, the cutoff rank was more competitive for the General category students as compared to the OBC category for MBBS admission. For other categories and rounds, use the provided filters.

AIIMS Jodhpur accepts the NEET UG entrance exam for selecting students to the MBBS course. Candidates with a valid score are then eligible for the NEET UG counselling. For 2026 admission, aspirants must refer to the table below to view some of the important NEET UG 2026 dates to be eligible for admission.

Candidates preparing for admission can refer to the table below to view or download the NEET UG round and year-wise consolidated cutoff list. This will help students to understand the past three years' cutoff trends.

The NEET PG cutoff 2024 was released for admission to the MD, MS, and PG Diploma courses across categories. During the last round of AIIMS Jodhpur MD cutoff 2024, the closing rank was 9471 for the students belonging to the General AI quota. Of all the MD specialisations, the top most competitive specialisation for admission was MD in Dermatology, Venerology & Leprosy for the general category.

AIIMS Jodhpur NEET PG cutoff 2024 was announced for MD courses. The cutoff was released as opening and closing ranks. As per the last round cutoff, MD in Dermatology, Venerology & Leprosy had the most stringent cutoff rank of 9471 for admission among the students belonging to the General AI category.
For other categories, rounds, and quotas, candidates can use the filters provided below:

NEET PG Counselling 2024 has been released for different categories of students. The special stray vacancy round result was the last round for NEET PG counselling. Candidates, who appeared in the counselling process can refer to the table below to view or download the consolidated year-wise NEET PG cutoff lists.
AIIMS Jodhpur INI CET cutoff 2026 has been released for the postgraduate courses MD, MS, etc. At Round 3, the closing ranks ranged from 33 to 27774 for students in the General AI quota.

Considering the ranks, the most competitive for admission was MD in General Medicine, with a lowest cutoff rank of 33. On the otherhand, the least competitive was MD in Anatomy, with INI CET 2026 cutoff rank of for admission in the General category.

In the table below, candidates can find the INI CET round- and category-wise cut-off details for admission to various PG medical courses. For General AI category students, the top three most competitive courses for admission were MD in General Medicine, MD in Paediatrics and MS in Obstetrtics and Gynaecology, with Round 3 closing ranks of 33, 334 and 362, respectively.

INI CET counselling is conducted in four rounds: mock round, first round, second round, and open round. All the rounds of counselling are conducted online. Candidates appearing for admission via INI CET can refer to the table provided below to view or download the round and year-wise INI CET cutoff lists.
AIIMS Jodhpur B.Sc. cutoff 2025 was 748 in the last round. In 2024, the last round closing rank for the same course was 966 for the students belonging to the General AI quota.
For the OBC category students, the AIIMS Nursing cutoff 2025 for the B.Sc. course was 1493 in the last round. For other categories and rounds, use the provided filters.

The AIIMS Jodhpur cutoff 2025 has been released based on the AIIMS Paramedical exam. The cutoff was released for different categories such as General, OBC, SC, ST and EWS categories belonging to the All India quota. The cutoff range among the General AI category was between 28 and 311.

The AIIMS Jodhpur cutoff 2024 has been released for MSc courses. During the Round 1 cutoff, the closing ranks for M.Sc. in Biochemistry is 5 for the candidates belonging to the General category under the AI quota.

reviews.review_heading: I just want to say that this college is just awesome.
reviews.review_text:
The central library is present, where every known book in MBBS is available and the digital library is also present. 
Classroom's infrastructure is just awesome and labs are also good and on the whole campus, high-speed Wi-Fi is available. 
In hostels, Wi-Fi is also available. 
Sports and games facilities are very good. Every known game court is present here; even an Olympic-size pool is also available.
The hostel is provided with double rooms having all the facilities.

reviews.review_heading: Review of AIIMS Jodhpur.
reviews.review_text: The college has splendid infrastructure and facilities. They provide Wi-Fi, e-classrooms, a central library, and labs with modern instruments. The college also provides double-seated rooms for UG students and a single-seater room for PG students. There is a sports complex and an activity hall for extracurricular activities. Mess and canteens are also available.

reviews.review_heading: Academics, faculty members, and classrooms are awesome.
reviews.review_text: In AIIMS there is a double seat rooms in room we were provided with a well furnished B.Ed chair study table and side small table The quality of food is quite fair and their is always a rotated menu every month Hostel registration is then along with academic admission New hostel rooms are affordable with a very small amount of rupees 990.

reviews.review_heading: Reviews by Dr. Vikas Saini.
reviews.review_text: The infrastructure is excellent. Labs are modernized. Instruments in labs are in good condition; their prices are in lakhs or crores. Classrooms also have projectors and all subject specimens like skeletons. Seats are in good condition. Hostel rooms are two-seater. A table, chair, bed, and wardrobe are also provided, and the mess menu is well-oriented.

reviews.review_heading: The best college for MD in Physical Medicine and Rehabilitation courses.
reviews.review_text: The infrastructural development is best organized here. All the facilities are good here except for education. The sports facilities at AIIMS Jodhpur are well-organized and advanced. The canteen and the mess are located in the campus. Medical facilities at Jodhpur AIIMS are the best.

reviews.review_heading: Overall very good college in all aspects.
reviews.review_text: There are various facilities available like lecture theatre, Wi-Fi, for practice we have different labs loke pathology, biochemistry, microbiology lab. In anatomy we have dissection hall. We have centralized AC library with Wi-Fi. We have e library also. The quality of hostel is good. There are single rooms connected by a passage of door. There is regular cleaning in hostels done. There is mess which provides good food and also canteen which provide food at reasonable range. There is new nescafe...

reviews.review_heading: I think AIIMS is one of the best colleges in India.
reviews.review_text: The hostels provided by the college are good. Our college provides 3 hostels, 1 for girls, 1 for boys and another one for PG students. About 2 more hostels are under construction. Nursing girls and MBBS girls are currently living in the same hostel.

reviews.review_heading: The name of AIIMS is enough.
reviews.review_text: Books and journals are always available for reading and can be issued by the students whenever required. All classrooms are completely Air conditioned, with smart classes and are in a theatre structure, so all the students get to SEE the faculty. Hostel rooms are well-equipped with beds, study tables and a wooden almirah. Food consists of chapati, dal rice and vegetable for dinner and lunch. For snacks and breakfast, they have options for milk, tea coffee and seasonal meals. All students get a h...

reviews.review_heading: Excellent internship opportunities and well-qualified professors.
reviews.review_text: Infrastructure at AIIMS Jodhpur was excellent. From fully equipped laboratories including microbiology, pathology to all high technology devices including a fully operational international radiology setup, the institute had cutting edge infrastructure. Hostels were really well built and maintained. Mess infrastructure was good, but food was average.

reviews.review_heading: Good Infrastructure and Facilities.
reviews.review_text: The college and hostel building is newly built so it is well furnished as of now. Centralised air conditioned lecture halls, auditoriums, exam hall, practical laboratories are there fully equipped with all the necessary instruments. Whole college and hospital building has a decent Wi-Fi connection. Hostels are really good and clean as they are newly constructed. Mess and canteen offer decent food. Facilities for all the sports is provided for the students.

reviews.review_heading: Our college has best infrastructure and facilities with enjoyment.
reviews.review_text: A new hostel for boys and girls was built this year. The Interior is excellent which gives a home-like feeling, Each room will be for 2 students provided with one bed, one study which is big enough, one small drawer, and one almirah. The hostel has 2 common bathrooms on each floor. There are 7 floors in both hostels, old hostel is single-seated, which is small but livable. Mess is provided with centralised air-conditioning with A+ food.

reviews.review_heading: It is a very good place for pursuing MBBS, if you are good enough to make it into this place.
reviews.review_text: Classrooms here are well constructed. The entire college and hospital have centralised air-conditioners and are having good cleanliness. Wi-Fi is available in the college. Library is available here and has a calm and peaceful environment which is a boon for a student. All types of sports are available here.

reviews.review_heading: Overall, AIIMS Jodhpur is the best college.
reviews.review_text: The infrastructure of AIIMS Jodhpur is the best. There are very expensive things in AIIMS Jodhpur. There are separate microscopes for every student, unlike that of state colleges. AIIMS Jodhpur is, no doubt, the best college to pursue MBBS.

reviews.review_heading: AIIMS Jodhpur is the best college.
reviews.review_text:
Infrastructural development is best organised here.
All the facilities other than studies are also good here.
Sports facilities are well organised and developed at AIIMS Jodhpur. Canteen and mess are located on the campus. Medical facilities are best at AIIMS Jodhpur.

reviews.review_heading: I think AIIMS is one of the best colleges in India and also in the world.
reviews.review_text: AIIMS Jodhpur provides students with excellent quality education. It has professional teachers with great knowledge in their respective fields. AIIMS is always investing in making the infrastructure one of the best in the country. AIIMS provides separate hostels for boys, girls and PG students. Single rooms with attached washrooms are available. Food provided in the mess is not similar to the food at home but better than anything else.

college_info.intro:
Among the different courses at SRM University a few stand out as the flagship programmes of the institute being its BTech in Computer Science and Engineering (CSE) and MBA programmes.
SRMIST University Kattankulathur Tamil Nadu is also known for its MBA programme, with state-of-the-art facilities and experienced faculty. The SRMIST MBA programme is extended for a duration of two years across a range of specialisations, some of which are shown here:
Another particular popular course choice are the Health Science programmes which are extended in the UG, PG, and Post-PG levels under a number of specialisations such as Pharmacy, Physiotherapy, Public Health, etc. Interested students can learn more about SRMIST Kattankulathur and its MSc programme below:

fees_and_eligibility.overview: SRM University extends a range of UG, PG and PhD level courses across the streams of Engineering, Management, Medicine & Health Sciences, Science, Humanities, Law, etc. with BTech, BArch, BCA, BCom, etc. at the UG level. The SRM University Kattankulathur BTech programme is extended across a range of specialisations including:

faqs.answer: A:    Yes, SRM University offers direct admission to certain programs to candidates who meet required eligibility criteria. The admission to these programs may include past academic performance, work experience, and other relevant factors.
faqs.question: Can a student join SRM IST without an entrance exam?

faqs.answer: A:    The deemed-to-be university accepts GATE score for M.Tech admission. However, aspirants who have not qualified GATE can also get admission to M.Tech course by qualifying SRMJEEE (PG) conducted by the university. SRM will conduct SRMJEEE in two phases. After the completion of the selection rounds, aspirants need to attend the counselling and document verification round. Only after the payment of course fee, admission is confirmed in the university.
faqs.question: Is GATE required for M.Tech admission at SRM Institute of Technology Kattankulathur?

faqs.answer: A:    The university fee structure is cumulative of several components, such as caution deposit, tuition fee, hostel fee, and more. A few of these components are one-time payable, while some have are charged annually/semesterly. SRM IST Chennai MBA tuition fee ranges between INR 1.9 Lacs and INR 11.5 lakh. Moreover, the one-time fee range between INR 1,200 and INR 11,000 and the hostel charges range between INR 1.45 Lacs and INR 3.3 lakh.The fee mentioned here is as per the official website/sanctioning body. It is still subject to changes and hence, is indicative.
faqs.question: What is the fee for SRM University MBA?

faqs.answer: A:    SRM Institute of Science and Technology admission is entrance-based for most of the courses. The Institute accepts national-level entrance exams like CAT, MAT, XAT, TANCET, NATA, SRMJEEE, SRMJEEM etc. for admission to MBA, BArch, BTech, M.Tech and other courses. Offering more than 80 courses and providing an annual intake of 52 K+, SRM IST offers admission based on the counselling through SRMJEEE e-counselling. To conclude, it is not very difficult to seek admission at SRM IST.
